heading: Mutiny and riot prohibited
label: 1792 Mutiny and riot prohibited
text: Whoever instigates, connives, willfully attempts to cause, assists, or conspires to cause any mutiny or riot, at any Federal penal, detention, or correctional facility, shall be imprisoned not more than ten years or fined under this title, or both.
notes: Historical and Revision NotesBased on title 18, U.S.C., 1940 ed., § 252 (, ).May 18, 1934, ch. 303, § 148 Stat. 782Escape provisions of this section were incorporated in .section 752 of this titleReference to persons causing, procuring, aiding and assisting was omitted. Such persons are principals under .section 2 of this titleMinor changes were made in translation and phraseology.

Amendments1994— substituted “fined under this title” for “fined not more than $25,000”.Pub. L. 103–3221986— inserted “, detention,” after “penal”.Pub. L. 99–6461984— substituted provisions deleting prohibition on bringing dangerous instrumentalities into prison and inserted provision setting forth a maximum $25,000 fine.Pub. L. 98–473

Effective Date of 1986 Amendment, , , provided that:Pub. L. 99–646, § 53(b)100 Stat. 3607Nov. 10, 1986“The amendment made by this section [amending this section] shall take effect 30 days after the enactment of this Act [].”Nov. 10, 1986
